message:
fix(storage): drain WAL via TRUNCATE checkpoint before reopen-after-rebuild

Follow-up to the post-rebuild reopen fix from the previous commit.
The reopen by itself was *necessary* but not always sufficient:
fsqlite still had committed frames sitting in the WAL when we
reopened, and the next VACUUM/REINDEX could trip over
"database is busy (snapshot conflict on pages: page N >
snapshot db_size M)" again — because the new connection inherited
the half-drained WAL state from the previous session.

src/storage/sqlite.rs::SqliteStorage::checkpoint_full
  New helper that issues `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` and
  falls back to `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)` if TRUNCATE fails
  (TRUNCATE requires the DB's internal exclusive lock, which is
  not always acquirable mid-mutation; PASSIVE is best-effort but
  always safe). Surfaces a `tracing::debug!` on the TRUNCATE
  fallback and returns an error only if even PASSIVE fails. The
  doc-comment is explicit that this is for *quiescent* call sites
  where the caller already holds the cross-process `.write.lock`,
  not for the hot path.

src/config/mod.rs::rebuild_database_family
  Insert a `storage.checkpoint_full()` call between
  `import_from_jsonl` and `storage.reopen_at(...)`. TRUNCATE
  failure is downgraded to `tracing::warn!` (with the same
  `db_path` field as the surrounding VACUUM/REINDEX warnings)
  because the reopen still recovers correctness — the warn just
  flags the case for operator visibility. Doc-comment for the
  block is rewritten to spell out the TRUNCATE-then-reopen pair.

Cargo.lock left modified intentionally — same local-sibling-path
patch override situation flagged in the previous beads_rust
passes.
